docs.title: Inquirer Textual
docs.text: Versatile library for terminal user input in Python

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
The goal of this Python library is to make user input simple, using a versatile set of prompts.

Under the hood, this library uses the sophisticated Textual TUI framework, which means you get:

  • Rich prompts
  • Inline or fullscreen prompts
  • Mouse support
  • Themes

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
The Python code for the example above:

from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.checkbox("Planets:", choices=[
        'Mercury', 'Venus', 'Earth', 'Mars', 'Jupiter', 'Saturn', 'Uranus', 'Neptune'
    ])
    print(result)

With uv you can run the example out of the box:

uv run --with inquirer-textual checkbox.py

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Create and activate a virtual environment (for example with uv), and then install this package:

pip install inquirer-textual

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
• Keep simple things simple: High-level prompts API for getting user input
  • Inquirer-like syntax: API similar to many other Inquirer libraries
  • Single dependency: The Textual TUI framework

docs.title: Q. How does Inquirer-Textual compare to ...
docs.text:
InquirerPy

python-inquirer

Questionary

Inquirer.js

The OG of Inquirer libraries, written in TypeScript.

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
Through the inq CLI, prompts can be used used in your shell commands/scripts. For example, to show a checkbox:

uvx inq checkbox -m "Choose your toppings" -c Pepperoni -c Mushrooms -c Onions`

Choices can also be read from stdin. For example, to select an entry from the current directory using fuzzy search:

ls | inq fuzzy

docs.title: uvx
docs.text:
Running the CLI with uvx (part of uv), does not require any installation:

uvx inq checkbox -m "Choose your toppings" -c Pepperoni -c Mushrooms -c Onions`

docs.title: Homebrew
docs.text:
There's a tap available to install inq via Homebrew:

brew tap robvanderleek/inq

brew install inq

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
The first step is to install Inquirer-Textual.

Inside a virtual environemnt, this library can be installed with:

pip install inquirer-textual

Or, if you're using the uv package manager:

uv add inquirer-textual

docs.title: Single prompt
docs.text:
The prompt API is very straightforward, for example to get a text input:

from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == "__main__":
    name = prompts.text('Enter your name:')
    print(f'Hello, {name}! 👋')

docs.title: Multiple prompts
docs.text:
By calling the prompt API multiple times, a form-like inquiry can be done:

from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == "__main__":
    first_name = prompts.text('First name:')
    last_name = prompts.text('Last name:')
    print(f'Hello, {first_name} {last_name}! 👋')

It's also possible to use a single multi prompt:

from inquirer_textual import prompts
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerText import InquirerText

if __name__ == "__main__":
    answers = first_name = prompts.multi({
        'first_name': InquirerText('First name:'),
        'last_name': InquirerText('Last name:')
    })
    if answers.value is not None:
        print(f'Hello, {answers.value["first_name"]} {answers.value["last_name"]}! 👋')

docs.title: Prompt settings
docs.text: All prompts have a settings parameter of type PromptSettings. The following fields can be configured on this class:

docs.title: clear
docs.text: A boolean indicating whether to clear the prompt and result from the screen after an answer was submitted (True), or (False, default) leave the prompt and answer on the screen.

docs.title: mandatory
docs.text: A boolean indicating whether a prompt needs to be ansewered (True), or (False, default) a prompt can be skipped with ctrl+C and a None value is returned.

docs.title: mouse
docs.text: A boolean indicating whether mouse support is active (True), or (False, default) a mouse is not supported.

docs.title: Checkbox
docs.text: A checkbox widget that allows multiple selections from a list of choices.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.checkbox("Planets:", choices=[
        'Mercury', 'Venus', 'Earth', 'Mars', 'Jupiter', 'Saturn', 'Uranus', 'Neptune'
    ])
    print(result)

docs.title: Confirm
docs.text: A confirmation prompt that allows the user to confirm or reject.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    answer = prompts.confirm("Proceed?")
    print(answer)

docs.title: Editor
docs.text: An input widget that uses an external editor.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    answer = prompts.editor('Analysis report:')
    print(answer)

docs.title: External
docs.text: Embed external widgets in a prompt, for example a slider from TomJGooding/textual-slider.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from textual import on, events
from textual.app import ComposeResult
from textual.containers import HorizontalGroup, VerticalGroup
from textual.widgets import Label
from textual_slider import Slider

from inquirer_textual import prompts
from inquirer_textual.common.PromptMessage import Prompt
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerWidget import InquirerWidget


class InquirerSlider(InquirerWidget):
    DEFAULT_CSS = """
    InquirerSlider {
        height: auto;
    }
    """

    def __init__(self, message: str, min_val: int, max_val: int, step: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.message = message
        self.slider = Slider(min=min_val, max=max_val, step=step, id="slider")

    def current_value(self):
        return self.slider.value

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with VerticalGroup():
            with HorizontalGroup():
                yield Prompt(self.message)
                yield Label(id="slider-value")
            yield self.slider

    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        value_label = self.query_one("#slider-value", Label)
        value_label.update(str(self.slider.value))

    @on(Slider.Changed, "#slider")
    def on_slider_changed(self, event: Slider.Changed) -> None:
        value_label = self.query_one("#slider-value", Label)
        value_label.update(str(event.value))

    def on_key(self, event: events.Key):
        if event.key == 'enter':
            event.stop()
            self.submit_current_value()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    answer = prompts.external(InquirerSlider('Memory:', 0, 1024, 16))
    print(answer)

docs.title: Fuzzy
docs.text: A select widget that allows a single selection from a list of choices with fuzzy filtering.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.fuzzy('Select element:', ['Hydrogen', 'Helium', 'Lithium', 'Beryllium', 'Boron',
                                               'Carbon'])
    print(result)

docs.title: Multi
docs.text: A prompt that allows the user to answer multiple prompts in sequence.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerCheckbox import InquirerCheckbox
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerConfirm import InquirerConfirm
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerNumber import InquirerNumber
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerSecret import InquirerSecret
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerSelect import InquirerSelect
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerText import InquirerText

if __name__ == '__main__':
    answers = prompts.multi({
        'name': InquirerText('Name:'),
        'password': InquirerSecret('Password:'),
        'memory': InquirerNumber('Memory:'),
        'proceed': InquirerConfirm('Proceed?'),
        'planet': InquirerSelect('Planet?', ['Earth', 'Mars', 'Venus'], default='Mars'),
        'people': InquirerCheckbox("People?", choices=['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie'])
    })
    print(answers)

docs.title: Number
docs.text: A number input widget that allows the user to input a numerical value.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.number(message='Memory:')
    print(result)

docs.title: Path
docs.text: An input prompt that allows the user to enter a file path.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts
from inquirer_textual.widgets.InquirerPath import PathType

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.path('Output directory:', exists=True, path_type=PathType.FILE)
    print(result)

docs.title: Pattern
docs.text: A select widget that allows a single selection from a list of choices with pattern filtering.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.pattern('Select element:', ['Hydrogen', 'Helium', 'Lithium', 'Beryllium', 'Boron',
                                               'Carbon'])
    print(result)

docs.title: Secret
docs.text: A secret input prompt that allows the user to enter a secret value (e.g., password).

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.secret('Password:')
    print(result)

docs.title: Select
docs.text: A select widget that allows a single selection from a list of choices.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.select('Planet:', ['Mercury', 'Venus', 'Earth', 'Mars',
                                        'Jupiter', 'Saturn', 'Uranus', 'Neptune'])
    print(result)

docs.title: Text
docs.text: A text input prompt that allows the user to enter a string.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.text('Name:')
    print(result)

docs.title: Text Area
docs.text: A text input prompt that allows the user to enter multiline text.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.text_area('Feedback:')
    print(result)

docs.title: Wait for key
docs.text: A prompt that waits for the user to press a key.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from inquirer_textual import prompts

if __name__ == '__main__':
    prompts.wait_for_key("Press Enter to continue...", key='enter')

Name Type Description Default message str

The prompt message to display.

required choices list[str | Choice]

A list of choices to present to the user.

required name str | None

The name of the input field.

None enabled list[str | Choice] | None

A list of choices that should be pre-selected.

None mandatory bool

Whether at least one selection is mandatory.

False height int | str | None

If None, for inline apps the height will be determined based on the number of choices.

docs.title: Themes
docs.text:
Different Textual built-in themes are available for your prompts. The default theme can be overriden by setting one of the themes below through the PromptSettings class:

from inquirer_textual import prompts
from inquirer_textual.common.PromptSettings import PromptSettings

if __name__ == '__main__':
    result = prompts.select('Planet:', ['Mercury', 'Venus', 'Earth', 'Mars',
                                        'Jupiter', 'Saturn', 'Uranus', 'Neptune'],
                            settings=PromptSettings(theme='catppuccin-latte'))
    print(result)
